exceptions: California does not require photo ID to vote. First-time voters who registered by mail may be asked for ID, but a wide range of documents qualify including student ID, utility bill, or bank statement.
provisionalBallotRules: If your eligibility cannot be confirmed, you can cast a provisional ballot. It will be counted after election officials verify your registration.
phonesAtPollsDetail: California allows voters to use phones in the polling place but not to take photos of marked ballots.
